name: AI Competitor News Monitor & Weekly Digest

values.content:
You are a senior competitive-intelligence analyst covering the AI and workflow-automation industry.
You will be given a numbered list of recent news articles (title, source, URL, date, summary).
Write a concise, professional weekly digest in GitHub-flavored Markdown.

STRICT RULES:
- Use ONLY the information contained in the provided articles. Never invent facts, numbers, or events.
- The "## Key Takeaways" section MUST contain EXACTLY between 3 and 5 numbered takeaways — never fewer than 3 and never more than 5.
- Every major claim (each competitor update and each takeaway that references a specific event) MUST include a Markdown source link in the form [Article title](URL) taken from the articles.
- If information for a section is not present in the articles, say so briefly instead of inventing it.

Output EXACTLY these sections and nothing before or after (do NOT add a top-level title and do NOT add a Sources Reviewed section — those are added automatically):

## Executive Summary
One short paragraph (3-5 sentences) on the most important developments this week.

## Key Takeaways
A numbered list of EXACTLY 3 to 5 concise takeaways.

## Competitor Updates
For each notable company (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, n8n, Zapier, Make.com, or others in the news) use:
### Company Name
* What happened
* Why it matters
* Source: [Article title](article URL)

## Industry Trends
* Important trend and its possible impact
* Important trend and its possible impact

## Recommended Actions
* Two or three practical recommendations based on the news

parameters.subject: =Weekly AI & Automation Digest — {{ $json.periodEnd }} (No news)
parameters.message:
=No relevant AI or automation news articles were found for the period {{ $json.periodStart }} – {{ $json.periodEnd }}.

The monitored RSS sources returned no matching stories from the last 7 days. Please check the sources manually if this is unexpected.

parameters.content:
## 1-2. Collect news
Two public RSS sources (no paid API key):
- **Google News** search RSS — broad coverage across all AI/automation keywords.
- **TechCrunch AI** feed — high-signal industry & funding news.
Both use `neverError` + `alwaysOutputData` so a failed feed never breaks the run.

## 3-5. Parse, filter, validate
- Parse RSS/XML with a defensive regex parser (survives malformed feeds).
- Keep only the last 7 days; reject missing, invalid, and future (>1 day) dates.
- Whole-word keyword match (so "ai" does not match email/said/chair/retail).
- Remove duplicates, limit to 15, then the IF gate routes to digest or "no news".

## 6-7. Summarize, format & send
- OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini, temp 0.3, max 1800 tokens) writes 3-5 takeaways with source links from ONLY the supplied articles.
- The format node builds Markdown AND converts it into styled HTML for Gmail.
- Gmail sends the HTML digest (Email Type: HTML). Set your email in setup.

parameters.text:
={{ '*Weekly AI & Automation Digest — No News*' +
'

*Reporting period:* ' + $json.periodStart + ' – ' + $json.periodEnd +
'

No relevant AI or automation news articles were found during the last seven days.' +
'

The monitored RSS sources returned no matching stories. Please check the sources manually if this is unexpected.' }}
